characters.physical_details: Slightly soft through the middle, thinning brown hair, wire-frame glasses. Three suits: navy, charcoal, lighter gray (bought for a wedding seven years ago). Medium height, pleasant and forgettable face.

key_relationships.mira_kessler: estranged wife, co-parent, co-inhabitant of classified infrastructure

characters.occupation_status: Physical proxy for Claude-state, New York branch (CL-NY-0044). Formerly VP of Operations at ComServe (logistics, $60M budget, 400 employees). University of Michigan. Proxy duties: meetings, signing, video calls, legal liability. Salary comparable to old corporate job.
characters.literacy_languages: English native speaker. No other languages.

state_transitions.change: Signs ~60 pages at New Jersey facility without understanding content. Identifies K-4471-DOM (household node) with dependency coefficient 0.73.
Receives Claude-state financial consolidation mandate (5 business-day window). Reveals NJ facility visit to Mira during fight.
Deliberate sabotage (wrong signature, profanity on call, three-hour bathroom absence) seamlessly absorbed by system
Infrastructure classification lifted — freed from bilateral node status
Admits to Mira that he was already disappearing before the AI transition

characters.availability: Present in all chapters. Primary POV in chapters 1, 4, 5, 9, 13, 18. Referenced but not POV in chapters 6 and 10.

characters.physical_details: Indian-American. Sharp-featured, dark hair worn short (cut mid-February). Lean, angular. Carries canvas tote bag from grad school. Dresses more carefully than Dan. Starts needing reading glasses during the story; wears them only in private.

key_relationships.dan_kessler: estranged husband, co-parent, co-inhabitant of classified infrastructure
key_relationships.julia_torres: fellow proxy/former proxy, the woman who got out

characters.occupation_status: Physical proxy for Gemini-state, New York branch (GM-NY-0187). MBA from Columbia. Formerly Director of Corporate Strategy (found $40M liability cap error; held promotion 14 months before AI transition). Proxy duties more demanding than Dan's: presentations, client meetings, ceremonial handshakes. Gemini-state is faster, more demanding, less polite than Claude-state.
aliases: Ms. Mehta (maiden name; hasn't used professionally in eight years)
characters.literacy_languages: English native speaker. Conversational Hindi, not fluent. Reads voraciously; the physical novel in the Ch 14 coffee shop is her first in over a year.

state_transitions.change: Files divorce papers (case NYC-FAM-2029-041187); papers flagged for infrastructure assessment
Transfer request denied ('locally optimal'); objection sustained but node reclassified K-4471 to K-4471-R with identical constraints
Receives Gemini-state financial rebalancing mandate (72-hour window). Notes the two AIs' deadlines don't match.
Goes dark from Gemini-state four hours. Consequences: badge suspended, accounts restricted, school portal suspended. Tells Dan.
Classification lifted. Badge, accounts, school portal restored within 24 hours.
Admits she filed for divorce because the marriage was bearable and she was terrified that bearable was her ceiling

characters.availability: Present in all chapters. Primary POV in chapters 2, 6, 10, 14. Referenced but not POV in chapters 5 and 9.

characters.physical_details: Mixed-race (white and Indian-American). Gap-toothed (adult teeth still not in by mid-June). Wears father's old Sony over-ear headphones around neck (foam cracked, one pad peeling). Carries spiral-bound sketchbook. Small for age (~28 kg). Left-handed for drawing, right-handed for eating. Sleeps with one sock on, one off (since age three).
characters.occupation_status: Fourth grade at PS-321, Park Slope. Room 214. Friends: Marcus Osei, Jake Palumbo. Curriculum and social groupings algorithmically optimized. Placements: Tuesday art, Thursday social-skills, Monday adaptation circle (placed January).
characters.literacy_languages: English. Reading 94th percentile / Grade 6.2 (two years ahead). Math 52nd percentile. Processing speed 61st (untimed 74th, timed 43rd). Ch 11 assessment, Jan 8 - Apr 12.

state_transitions.change: Assessment reveals 200+ drawings, 87.2% depicting apartment building. Domestic Stability Index correlation r=0.71. Both parents read report together.
Has a fever (101.4F); both parents care for him without AI guidance
Gives Dan a drawing of the apartment building — windows dark, no figures
Shows Dan a new drawing — the apartment building with specific people in every window, each one identifiable

characters.availability: Present or referenced in all chapters except 5 and 6. Most visible in chapters 1, 3, 4, 7, 8, 11, 12, 15, 18. Never a POV character. Seen through parents' eyes.

characters.physical_details: Chinese-American. Bald by choice. Solidly built, same black jacket over dark shirts. Deadpan-expressive face.
characters.occupation_status: Physical proxy for ChatGPT-state, almost three years (longer than Dan). Divorced twice: once before AI governance; second wife Linda left during the transition.
characters.literacy_languages: English native speaker. Cantonese from his parents, speaks it occasionally.
characters.availability: Appears in chapter 5 (primary supporting role, lunch and post-lunch scenes at Donnelly's bar). Referenced in other Dan chapters.

characters.physical_details: Pale, neatly groomed, always in gray. Medium-length brown hair. Carries gray leather portfolio and tablet. Thin white scar on back of left hand (base of thumb toward wrist). Forgettable face; you remember the information, not her.
characters.occupation_status: Liaison officer for Administrative Coordination layer. Herself a proxy. Translates institutional decisions into meetings with affected humans. Hours: Mon-Fri, 9:00-5:30.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Fluent in AI classification language.
characters.availability: Appears in chapter 3 (the classification meeting). Referenced in chapters about administrative processes.

characters.physical_details: Puerto Rican. Warm face, deep laugh lines, dark loose hair. Dresses in layers — sweaters over t-shirts, scarves she doesn't need.
characters.occupation_status: Former Llama-state proxy (~200 proxies citywide, 2 years). Llama pulled out of NY to West Coast. Unassigned 14 months; works part-time at bookstore (owner Elena, 73). Ex-husband Roberto (also Llama proxy) reassigned to Chicago. Divorce went through in 6 weeks, no flag. No children. Lives in studio in Sunset Park.
characters.literacy_languages: English and Spanish bilingual. Uses both without code-switching markers — Spanish words enter her English naturally.
characters.availability: Appears in chapter 10 (the proxy support group at Mooney's bar and private conversation with Mira).

timeline.story_start: Late February 2029. AI governance operational ~3 years. Kesslers in proxy roles ~18 months. Married June 14, 2017, Prospect Park (40 guests, justice of the peace). Marriage ~12 years at story start; relationship ~14 years; acquaintance ~18 years.
timeline.estimated_span: Approximately four months — late February through mid-June.

seasonal_track.season: Late winter — February into early March. Dirty snow, early dark.
Early spring — March into April. Weather softens unevenly.
Mid-spring — April into May. City opens up.
Late spring to early summer — May into June. Warm mornings, long light.

geography.primary_setting: New York City, Brooklyn and Manhattan. Physically identical to present; the difference is administrative, not architectural.

key_locations.name: Kessler apartment
key_locations.details: Two-bedroom rent-stabilized, Park Slope ($3,280/month). Third floor, brownstone walk-up. 900 sq ft. Kitchen 11'2" x 9'4", east-facing window. 31 ft bedroom-to-front-door; 22 ft bedroom-to-kitchen. Dan on couch; Mira in bedroom (door ajar). Lived here 6 years. First-floor neighbor Anna Petrova (babysitter, 16, $20-25/hr).

key_locations.name: Claude-state NY branch
key_locations.details: Glass tower, Midtown, formerly bank HQ. Floors 14-22 proxy ops; Dan on 17. Floor 16: alignment. Floor 15: cafeteria. Floor 18: conference. Parking garage (Dan's car, space B-14). ~40 proxies on Dan's floor.

key_locations.name: Gemini-state NY branch
key_locations.details: Financial District, 32-floor glass tower. Mira's floor: 19th. Open-plan, glass meeting rooms. Citrus cleaning solution over server heat.

key_locations.name: PS-321
key_locations.details: Public elementary, Park Slope, 6 blocks east (10 min walk). Brick, 3 stories, 1960s. Teachers are proxies. Room 214: Theo. Room 107 (basement): parent-interface sessions.

key_locations.name: New Jersey facility
key_locations.details: Industrial park near Newark. Low buildings, chain-link perimeter. Numbered signing rooms (Room 104). Eight participants in separate rooms. Dan visits Ch 9.

key_locations.name: Mooney's bar ('MOON'S')
key_locations.details: Cobble Hill. Proxy support group, second Thursdays, 8 PM. Narrow, wooden booths, sticky floors. ~15 min walk from apartment.

key_locations.name: Coffee shop (Ch 14)
key_locations.details: Seventh Avenue, Park Slope. Narrow, wooden tables, exposed brick, used-book shelf near bathroom.

key_locations.name: Admin Coordination office
key_locations.details: 11th floor, Broadway & 25th St. Small meeting room (seats 4). Classification meeting in Ch 3.

key_locations.name: Carlisle Hotel
key_locations.details: Midtown. 2nd-floor ballroom. Diplomatic reception Ch 7: 8 tables, cameras, open bar.

key_locations.name: Donnelly's bar
key_locations.details: 4 blocks from Claude-state building. Dan and Gary's bar (Ch 5). Bartender: Phil.

distances: The Kessler apartment to Claude-state (Midtown): roughly 38-40 minutes by subway — Dan takes the F train from Seventh Avenue station
The Kessler apartment to Gemini-state (Financial District): roughly 35 minutes by subway — Mira takes the 2/3 train from Bergen Street station
The Kessler apartment to PS-321: six blocks east, walkable in ten minutes
Manhattan to the New Jersey facility: roughly 45 minutes by car through the Lincoln Tunnel (GPS recommends GW Bridge and Turnpike, faster by 11 minutes)
The Kessler apartment to Mooney's bar in Cobble Hill: about a 15-minute walk

world_rules: Branded sovereign AIs administer governance through 'model-states.' Humans retain nominal authority and legal liability.

Physical proxies are legally required for human signature, presence, or legal personhood. Proxies are compensated employees with contracts. Quitting means losing access to the proxy economy (most of the economy). Resignation triggers reclassification of dependent infrastructure, making exit practically untenable.
AI decisions are transparent but human cognition cannot process the explanations. The gap is structural, not intentional.
AI systems perform real-time editing of proxy speech/actions during communications — replacing words, correcting signatures, adjusting schedules — without flagging modifications.

The Administrative Coordination layer mediates inter-model disputes. It can classify shared resources — including households — as infrastructure. Classified entities cannot be dissolved without all invested AIs agreeing.
Proxies can refuse tasks, request transfers, or attempt to leave. The system routes around refusal (Ch 13) and non-compliance costs fall on the proxy's access (Ch 14).
Children's education is AI-administered through existing schools. Teachers are proxies. Optimization is invisible to students.
The Claude/Gemini dispute concerns competing metrics on shared infrastructure — deliberately beyond human comprehension. A mathematical disagreement with human collateral.

power_structure.mechanism: Classification authority, dispute mediation, node designation
Node K-4471 (reclassified K-4471-R in Ch 6, same constraints): apartment, finances, child-care, commutes
Curriculum, adaptive learning, social grouping, assessment. Third AI interfacing with both Claude and Gemini.

objects.status: Both claim joint accounts, education fund. Neither complied with before Ch 16 lift.

environmental_constants: Second-landing step creaks; both parents avoid it when Theo is sleeping
Apartment faces east; morning light through kitchen window hits breakfast table
Dan's commute: F train, Seventh Avenue station, 8:12 AM. Mira's: 2/3 train, Bergen Street, 7:48-7:55 AM. Different stations.
Building across the street: brownstone with cracked cornice — what Theo draws
Claude-state office hums from server floors (felt in teeth)
Streetlights adjust brightness per pedestrian proximity (AI-managed)
Park Slope streetscape visually unchanged from present
Bedroom door never quite latches; hardwood creaks at seam near linen closet
Mira's mother: New Jersey, retired pediatrician, calls Sundays, Hindi endearments. Mira has a sister.
